dialogos.texto_en: What's up?\nYou're spacing out while staring at the sea.
Well, the sea is really beautiful.\nI could look at it forever.
It's sparkling like jewels.\nAs expected of the sea in Okinawa!
I've heard of a saying that the sea in Okinawa is so beautiful,\na color was named after it%p-2;――
Seeing it in person, I can definitely understand why.
A color named after the sea?
Yeah, it seems to be named after the place name.\nIt's surprising that even in Okinawa,\nthere are such differences.
When I hear that kind of story,\nI want to go to various seas around the world.\nI wonder what colors there are.
The famous ones are Kerama Blue\nand Hateruma Blue, I think.
Oh, I've heard of Hateruma Blue before.
It refers to Hateruma Island, right?
Yeah, that's right.\nAnd Kerama refers to Kerama Island.
I'd like to see Hateruma Blue.\nCan't we go there this time?
I wonder.\nHateruma Island is Japan's southernmost\ninhabited island, so it might be far.
The southernmost point...!
That's right, it's really far.
I found out about it when I was planning my trip,\nbut Hateruma Island is quite far from Naha.
Okinawa is bigger than I thought.
It might be a difficult journey,\nbut it'll be even more impressive\nto see Hateruma Blue.
I've been curious too,\nso I was thinking of putting it\non my list of places to visit.
It seems you can only go by boat.\nIf the sea is rough, there's nothing we can do.
That's a shame.\nI really wanted to go.
Well, it can't be helped.\nIt's hard to fit it into our busy schedule.
Yes, depending on the condition of the sea,\nit's possible that we won't be able to\ntravel there for weeks on end.
Hateruma Blue...\nI'd like to see its beauty with my own eyes someday.
It reminds me of last summer...\nHearing about sea conditions.
Ahaha...\nWe were stranded on the island\nbecause the sea was so rough.
That's right.\nI seriously thought,\n'what's going to happen to us?'
If we had made one mistake,\nit would have been a serious situation.
But, thanks to Uesugi-san,\nit became a really good memory.
Yes, an unforgettable, and precious, memory.
It's something to look forward to next time.
That's right.\nNext time, we could visit Okinawa for the sole purpose \nof going to Hateruma island.
Well, then, let's aim to go this summer!\nLet's start planning right away!
Hey, hey, hold on a minute.\nYou're jumping the gun a bit, aren't you?
But if we plan it now,\nit will definitely turn out to be a wonderful trip!
If we decide on a schedule in advance,\nwe can also make the trip longer, you know?
The longer we stay in Okinawa,\nthe more chances we have to visit Hateruma Island.\nLet's plan to stay for a whole month!
A whole month is a bit long...\nI think it's difficult to adjust the schedule\neven if we start now, you know?
Well, a month might be too long,\nbut if we make a promise for the next trip,\nwe can travel like this again.
What's wrong? Are you okay?
Oh, I'm just a little surprised.\nI never thought I would hear such words from you,\nUesugi-kun.
But, I agree.\nMaking a promise for the next trip so we can\nall come together again...it's a wonderful thing.
Still, for now, why don't we enjoy\nthe ocean in front of us?\nWe can talk about the next trip at the hotel.
Yeah, you're right.
Ah.\nWe've been quiet for a while.
Yeah.
It's funny how sometimes you feel like\nit's a waste to talk.
Ah! It's that I don't want to talk\nto you two or anything like that!
Don't worry!\nI sort of understand what you're trying to say.
Today, the weather is great. If the weather\nhad been bad, we wouldn't have been able to see\nsuch a beautiful ocean.
The sea...\nNature is truly mysterious.\nIt can show such a beautiful sight%p-2;――
And then suddenly,\nit can become so rough that ships can't sail.
You never know what the ocean's thinking.
Well, dealing with nature\nhas always been difficult, no matter the era.
That's probably why it's beautiful.
That's right!\nNext time we come to Okinawa, let's pray to the ocean\nso that it will be nice and sunny.
Teehee, that's a good idea.
I hope our prayers reach it.
